qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Custom component preview API executes attacker supplied Python for any active user
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding reaches create_class() and prepare_global_scope() through POST /custom_component and POST /custom_component/update, while the advisory targets POST /validate/code invoking validate_code() on attacker-supplied code.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: File upload API allows path traversal and arbitrary local file write for authenticated users
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This is a v2 file-upload path traversal in the local storage backend, not the advisory's validate endpoint exec path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: MCP resource read exposes cross-tenant files and arbitrary local file reads
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an authenticated MCP resource-read and storage path issue, not attacker-supplied Python reaching validate_code() on the validate route.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Monitor endpoints let authenticated users read and tamper with other users' records
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a cross-tenant authorization failure in monitor routes and does not involve the advisory's validate endpoint or exec_globals code-evaluation path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Build API lets any authenticated user execute another user's private flow by UUID
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an authorization bug in the build endpoint for victim flow UUIDs, not the validate route's direct code-execution behavior.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Webhook endpoint runs arbitrary flows without authentication by default
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an unauthenticated webhook-triggered flow-execution issue; it may indirectly reach user-configured components, but it does not identify the advisory's POST /validate/code -> validate_code() root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code/root cause. CVE-2026-0770 is the validate-endpoint bug where POST /validate/code calls validate_code() and executes attacker-supplied code via exec(); this session's qualifying findings cover different routes and control-flow conditions.
evidence: The advisory JSON and ZDI reference both describe the validate endpoint and handling of exec_globals in src/lfx/src/lfx/custom/validate.py as the vulnerable root cause.
Langflow's validate router maps POST /validate/code to validate_code(), but none of the six qualifying session findings reports that route or function in its exploit path.

The closest session finding, custom_component_preview_rce, reaches create_class() and prepare_global_scope() through custom-component preview/update APIs, which is related unsafe code evaluation in the same component family but a different function and entrypoint.

The public CVE-2026-0770 PoC material separately lists custom_component, custom_component/update, webhook, and build_public_tmp as other related vulnerable endpoints, which supports treating the session's custom-component and webhook findings as distinct open-world issues rather than the advisory's own root cause.
